title: Happy Hour

jokes.text: Not counting the last one, 25. / Count the last one. / Okay, 25 and one girl pushup.
jokes.setup: Employee asks for count after pushup challenge
jokes.punchline: Michael's dismissive counting reveals one was a 'girl pushup'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael undermining a moment of achievement with casual dismissiveness

dialogue.speaker: Employee
dialogue.line: How many did you do?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Fifty-seven.

dialogue.speaker: Employee
dialogue.line: That's impressive.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, one of them was a girl pushup, so really fifty-six.

jokes.text: Anyone who does more than 25 pushups... And one girl pushup. ...gets to go home.
jokes.setup: Michael offers incentive for beating the record
jokes.punchline: Employee inserts the dismissive 'girl pushup' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Callback that reinforces the running gag about the pushup standards

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Alright, if anyone can beat the record, I will personally give them a hundred dollar bonus.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'll do a hundred girl pushups.

jokes.text: I say 'dance,' they say 'how high?'
jokes.setup: Michael trying to demonstrate his authority
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'jump' with 'dance' in the classic phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael malapropism - sounds authoritative but reveals his confusion

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay everyone, listen up. I'm in charge here, and I need you all to know that you need to jump on board with my new initiative.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Jump on board?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yes. How high can you jump? Because that's how committed you need to be.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Did you mean 'get on board'?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I know what I said. You gotta jump. It's like that saying — if I told you to jump, you'd ask how high. Well, I'm telling you to dance, so...

jokes.text: Essentially what we have here is one of those stories where a mother lifts a car to save her baby.
jokes.setup: Stanley doing pushups beyond his usual capability
jokes.punchline: Michael's overwrought comparison to superhuman strength stories
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to turn mundane moments into epic narratives

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wow, Stanley! You're doing pushups like you're some kind of superhero. What are you, bitten by a radioactive fitness instructor? Did you drink gamma radiation smoothies? Are you training to fight crime at night?

jokes.text: He's got all this weight that's helping him go down.
jokes.setup: Stanley's impressive pushup performance
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual observation about Stanley's weight advantage
jokes.explanation: Jim's characteristic dry observation delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he's got like 60 pounds on me, so...

jokes.text: All right, Papa Bear.
jokes.setup: Stanley just completed an exhausting physical challenge
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate pet name in the moment
jokes.explanation: Michael's awkward familiarity with Stanley creates discomfort

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: No. I always come in at 7:00. / No you don't.
jokes.setup: Oscar claims consistent early arrival time
jokes.punchline: Matt immediately contradicts him, creating awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective awkward moment from being caught in a fib

jokes.text: Yeah, we talked this morning, and we talked at Christmas, so a little momentum there.
jokes.setup: Oscar trying to build connection with Matt
jokes.punchline: Two conversations across months constitutes 'momentum'
jokes.explanation: Oscar's overly analytical approach to romance is perfectly captured

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Two conversations across months constitutes 'momentum'

jokes.text: Has that ever happened? Ever? / Didn't we? I think we did.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests happy hour as if it's a tradition
jokes.punchline: Jim points out it's never happened; Michael's vague false memory
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to invent traditions and Jim's reality check

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We should do happy hour. You know, like we always do.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: We've never done that.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Sure we have. Remember? Last month? That place with the... drinks?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That didn't happen.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, we should make it a tradition then.

jokes.text: You can be gay with Matt, just be straight with me.
jokes.setup: Discussing Oscar's interest in Matt
jokes.punchline: Michael's unintentional wordplay mixing orientations with honesty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant accidental wordplay that reveals Michael's awkwardness around gay topics

jokes.text: I tend to wear something low-cut, get men to flirt with me, and Bob beats them up.
jokes.setup: Discussion about going to bars with Bob
jokes.punchline: Phyllis casually describes using herself as bait for violence
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Phyllis moment - sweet delivery of disturbing content

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Bob and I used to go to bars all the time. I'd wear a short skirt, low-cut top, really put myself out there. Then when the guys would come up and start harassing me, Bob would jump in and beat the crap out of them. He was my hero.

jokes.text: I gotta tell you, this baby is amazing. She gets me out of everything. And I love her. I also love her very much.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his excuse strategy
jokes.punchline: Catching himself sounding callous and overcompensating
jokes.explanation: Jim's self-awareness about sounding like a bad parent creates the humor

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So I told her I couldn't come to her thing because I had to work late. Then I realized how that sounded, so I added that I was thinking about her the whole time.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Which, in retrospect, made it worse.

jokes.text: I did not see this coming.
jokes.setup: Pam's explosive enthusiasm about going out
jokes.punchline: Jim's understated reaction to her excitement
jokes.explanation: Jim's dry delivery contrasts perfectly with Pam's over-enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Jim! We're going out tonight! I am so excited I could just explode!

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah... cool.

jokes.text: Talk to me that way again and I'll cut your face off. / Whoa!
jokes.setup: Andy speaking flirtatiously to Angela
jokes.punchline: Angela's violent threat followed by Andy's startled reaction
jokes.explanation: Angela's extreme reaction to mild flirtation is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You know, Angela, I've always thought you were really special.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: If you don't back away from me right now, I will stab you with this pen.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay! Okay, I'm going!

jokes.text: That's actually pretty funny, but in general, you know... Quiet.
jokes.setup: Andy apparently making noise during their talking head
jokes.punchline: Angela's begrudging admission followed by immediate shutdown
jokes.explanation: Angela's controlling nature even extends to their confessionals

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: He's very loud and obnoxious, and it's distracting.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I can hear you.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Well, when a new mom hears a baby cry, her you-know-whats fill up with you know what, and then her shirt gets, you know... That would be funny.
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to explain lactation euphemistically
jokes.punchline: His awkward delivery and thinking it would be 'funny'
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin inappropriate commentary delivered with his characteristic cluelessness

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: So, uh, when a woman's body is producing milk, you know, for the baby, that's... that's lactation. But I like to call it 'the milky way.' Get it? Because milk? And the Milky Way? I think that's pretty funny.

jokes.text: That would be sublime.
jokes.setup: Being asked to be fourth for pool
jokes.punchline: Dwight's overly formal response to casual invitation
jokes.explanation: Dwight's tendency to use unnecessarily elevated language for simple situations

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I would be honored to be your fourth. However, I must inform you that I am a skilled player. I have won three consecutive pool tournaments at the Schrute Farms community center. I trust you understand the competitive level at which I operate.

jokes.text: I'll call this a deck if it'll make you happy, but this is just a porch without a roof.
jokes.setup: Someone showing off their deck construction
jokes.punchline: Darryl's blunt assessment disguised as politeness
jokes.explanation: Great construction humor with Darryl's matter-of-fact delivery

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: That's a nice deck. I'm sure the termites are gonna love it.

jokes.text: Pam and Jim work for me, and if they win, they are fired. / I should hope not. / No. No. Not really. Not really. But they better not win.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to be impressive on his date
jokes.punchline: His immediate backtracking when Julie shows concern
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at playful authority immediately undermined by needing approval

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm a really good driver. I've only had like three accidents.

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: Three accidents?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, four if you count the one where I hit a parked car. But that was parked illegally, so technically that was their fault.

jokes.text: A girl like me is why a guy like you comes to a place like this. / Ooh. I love repartee. / Do you? / Usually means there's a battle scene coming.
jokes.setup: Flirtatious exchange between Isabel and Dwight
jokes.punchline: Dwight's unexpected interpretation of repartee as combat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - taking romantic banter in a completely literal/martial direction

dialogue.speaker: Isabel
dialogue.line: You're quite the wordsmith, Dwight.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I appreciate that. You have a sharp wit yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Isabel
dialogue.line: Well, I do enjoy a good verbal sparring match.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Sparring? I'll get my nunchucks.

jokes.text: See, I didn't think you could teach that. I thought that was something you were born with. / What am I thinking right now? / Are you thinking that I said ESP? / Yes. I feel like an idiot.
jokes.setup: Michael responding to Isabel being an ESL teacher
jokes.punchline: His confusion between ESL and ESP, then feeling foolish
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael confusion with good setup and payoff structure

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, ESP? Like mind reading?

dialogue.speaker: Isabel
dialogue.line: No, ESL. English as a Second Language.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh. Right. Yeah, I knew that.

jokes.text: I was a little nervous when Pam told me he was her boss, but he doesn't act like a boss at all. If I had a boss like that, we'd never get anything done.
jokes.setup: Isabel commenting on Michael's casual demeanor
jokes.punchline: Her unintentionally accurate assessment of Michael's management
jokes.explanation: Outsider's perspective accidentally nails the central problem with Michael

dialogue.speaker: Isabel
dialogue.line: He's like a golden retriever in a business suit. All enthusiasm, no actual training.

jokes.text: Well, apparently Michael Scott is on a date, and that... That, my friend, changes everything.
jokes.setup: Jim observing Michael's date situation
jokes.punchline: His ominous prediction about Michael dating
jokes.explanation: Jim's foreboding about Michael's romantic behavior sets up expectations

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Michael's gonna end up dating someone from the office.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It's gonna be a disaster.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Date Mike. Nice to meet me. How do you like your eggs in the morning?
jokes.setup: Michael putting on a hat and adopting a persona
jokes.punchline: Introducing himself as 'Date Mike' with immediate sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Iconic Michael moment - the birth of Date Mike with perfectly cringe pickup line

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Date Mike. Nice to meet me. How do you like your eggs in the morning?

jokes.text: Do you want to just make a run for it? / Maybe.
jokes.setup: Michael's Date Mike performance
jokes.punchline: Julie's polite but clear desire to escape
jokes.explanation: Natural reaction to Michael's behavior - understated but effective

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm Date Mike. Milk's favorite guy.

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: That's... that's great. I think I'm going to go.

jokes.text: Mommy!
jokes.setup: Michael trying to be impressive at an arcade
jokes.punchline: Childish exclamation during what should be adult socializing
jokes.explanation: Michael's regression to childhood during adult social situations

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm gonna crush this game. Watch and learn, people.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yes! Yes! I'm the best! I'm the best!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Who's the man? Who's the man? I'm the man!

jokes.text: Stop telling me how to spend my tickets. / They'd be like, 'What's up with those two?' 'Hey, guys, get a bedroom already.' 'Did we miss the wedding?'
jokes.setup: Angela and Andy trying to appear casual while secretly dating
jokes.punchline: Their imagined reactions are more romantic than their actual behavior
jokes.explanation: Angela and Andy's delusion about how obvious their relationship is

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: We should probably just act normal around the office.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, totally normal. No one will suspect a thing.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I'll just ignore you completely, and you can pretend I don't exist.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Perfect. And when our eyes accidentally meet across the conference room, I won't gaze longingly into your soul.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: And I won't feel my heart skip a beat.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: This is going to be so easy.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: The easiest thing we've ever done.

jokes.text: Three brothers. Really? Two are in the Marines, one's a cop. / Vegetarian? / No. I love meat. / What's your blood type? / O negative. Universal donor.
jokes.setup: Michael's increasingly rapid-fire questions on the date
jokes.punchline: The progression from normal to medical/logistical questions
jokes.explanation: Michael's dating technique resembles a job interview or medical screening

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So, Julie, where are you from?

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: Ohio.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh, Ohio! Great state. What do your parents do?

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: My dad's an accountant, my mom's a teacher.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Accountant and teacher, fantastic. Do you have any siblings?

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: Two brothers.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Two brothers. Any major health issues in your family? Genetic disorders? High cholesterol? Any history of mental illness? What's your credit score? Do you have a valid passport? Can you lift fifty pounds? What's your stance on vaccines?

jokes.text: Angela versus Isabel. Height, advantage Isabel. Birthing hips, advantage Isabel. Remaining childbearing years, advantage Isabel. Legal obligation, advantage Angela.
jokes.setup: Dwight comparing his two romantic interests
jokes.punchline: Clinical analysis ending with the contract obligation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight analysis - treating romance like agricultural breeding with legal twist

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Angela has the loyalty of a German Shepherd and the grace of a Scandinavian ski instructor. But Esther has youth on her side. However, Angela and I have a prenuptial agreement that clearly states if I leave her for a younger woman, I forfeit my beet farm and my title as Assistant Regional Manager.

jokes.text: I'm tying a knot in the stem with my tongue. / Michael, you don't have to do this.
jokes.setup: Michael attempting to show off with a cherry stem
jokes.punchline: Julie's gentle but desperate plea for him to stop
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward Michael performance with Julie's polite horror

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Watch this, I can tie a cherry stem in a knot with my tongue.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's a sign of a good kisser.

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: Please stop.

jokes.text: I would say I kind of have an unfair advantage, because I watch reality dating shows like a hawk. And I learn. I absorb information from the strategies of the winners and the losers. Actually, I probably learn more from the losers.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his dating expertise
jokes.punchline: Admission that he learns more from the losers
jokes.explanation: Michael's unintentional self-awareness about learning from dating show failures

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, I've dated a lot of women. And I've learned something from each and every one of them. But honestly, I think I've learned the most from the ones who didn't want to date me.

jokes.text: This is exactly what I don't want. The drama. I don't want the drama! / You love drama. / I know, I do, right? I'm a total drama queen.
jokes.setup: Angela claiming to hate drama
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting she loves drama when called out
jokes.explanation: Angela's self-contradiction and immediate acceptance of the truth

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I hate drama. I really do.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Angela, you literally create drama every single day.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I love it. I live for it. Are you happy?

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, no. I want a big family. Tall, thick. A big, physically big family.
jokes.setup: Isabel suggesting she could see enjoying a big family
jokes.punchline: Dwight's clarification about physical size rather than number
jokes.explanation: Dwight's agricultural/breeding approach to family planning

dialogue.speaker: Isabel
dialogue.line: I could see enjoying a big family.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Big like in number, or big like in physical size? Because I'm gonna need you to be more specific.

jokes.text: Wow! Maybe we should tell her he's not normally like this. / Maybe it should come from a man. / Maybe it should come from a note. / With flowers. Tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Concern about Michael's behavior on his date
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate plans to apologize remotely
jokes.explanation: Jim and Pam's collaborative damage control planning

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: We need to do something about Michael's date.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What do you mean? Let him figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: No, he's going to say something weird. We should send him a text.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: A text? Pam, he's on a date.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Exactly. We'll tell him what to say. I'll write it out, you send it from a fake number so he thinks it's advice.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Okay, better idea. We dress up like waiters and interrupt with a special announcement about how great he is.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: We hire a skywriter. We get a plane—

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Pam, stop.

jokes.text: Yeah! Why don't you send the bill to 23 I Don't Care Lane, Scranton, Pennsylvania?
jokes.setup: Bar manager mentioning pool table re-felting cost
jokes.punchline: Michael's sassy fake address response
jokes.explanation: Michael's bratty response to authority figures

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: We got a problem? / Yes. Homelessness.
jokes.setup: Confrontational 'Do we have a problem?' question
jokes.punchline: Michael's unexpected pivot to social issues
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - escalates confrontation then completely deflates it

dialogue.speaker: Bartender
dialogue.line: Do we have a problem?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, we do. I think we need to talk about income inequality. The top one percent now controls more wealth than the entire middle class combined. It's unconscionable.

jokes.text: Well, it just so happens that I'm a manager, too, and the way I manage people is that I touch their hearts and souls with humor, with love and maybe a dash of razzle-dazzle, and I don't see that from you.
jokes.setup: Bar manager asserting authority
jokes.punchline: Michael's overwrought management philosophy speech
jokes.explanation: Michael's grandiose self-image in contrast to the bar situation

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, listen up everybody. A bar manager has to be firm but fair. You gotta lay down the law, but you also gotta be their friend. It's a delicate balance. Like a tightrope walker, but instead of a rope, it's management philosophy, and instead of a walker, it's me.

jokes.text: If you're really interested, it is called Somehow I Manage, and there's going to be a picture of me on the cover shrugging with my sleeves rolled up.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he's writing a management book
jokes.punchline: Specific description of his posed cover photo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion with vivid, specific imagery of his fake book

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm writing a management book. The working title is 'Somehow I Manage.' And my cover photo, I'm on a motorcycle. But I'm not wearing a helmet. I'm wearing a halo.

jokes.text: Read it? I own it. But no, I have not read it.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's read Lee Iacocca's book
jokes.punchline: Claims ownership but admits never reading it
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to claim expertise he doesn't have

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, I own it. I haven't read it, but I own it.

jokes.text: I own a Chrysler. / Shut up. / No, you shut up.
jokes.setup: Michael's recommendation backfires when manager already owns a Chrysler
jokes.punchline: Devolves into playground-level argument
jokes.explanation: Michael's regression to childish behavior when challenged

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, maybe you just don't know how to drive it properly.

dialogue.speaker: Bar Manager
dialogue.line: I know how to drive, genius. It's the car that's the problem.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what someone with a broken car would say.

dialogue.speaker: Bar Manager
dialogue.line: Oh, so now I'm broken?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, your car is broken. Which means you're broken for buying it.

dialogue.speaker: Bar Manager
dialogue.line: You're the one who told me to buy it!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, you're the one who listened to me!

jokes.text: Go, go. Just sit down next to a beautiful woman and start talking to myself to confuse other people.
jokes.setup: Andy following through on their plan to hide their relationship
jokes.punchline: His bizarre interpretation of 'throwing people off the scent'
jokes.explanation: Andy's literal interpretation of their deception plan

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, so we're going to throw people off the scent by acting completely normal around each other.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm going to start wearing a woman's perfume to confuse them.

jokes.text: We didn't say we were gonna, like, start groping strangers. / I was flirting with a man.
jokes.setup: Angela horrified by Andy's behavior
jokes.punchline: Andy's matter-of-fact correction about his target
jokes.explanation: Andy's obliviousness to appropriate behavior boundaries

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: What are you doing?!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What? I'm just trying to hit my targets.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Your targets?!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, my sales targets. What did you think I meant?

jokes.text: I did. I actually put it in your bowl.
jokes.setup: Asked if he has a business card
jokes.punchline: Claims he put it in someone's fishbowl (presumably for a drawing)
jokes.explanation: Michael's confusion about business networking at a bar

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I have a card. I put it in someone's fishbowl for a drawing.

jokes.text: Whoa! A lot of Stanley Hudsons in here.
jokes.setup: Bartender pulls out Stanley's business card from fishbowl
jokes.punchline: Michael's attempt to cover his mistake
jokes.explanation: Michael's weak attempt to explain away the business card mix-up

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh, that's not a business card. That's actually a withdrawal slip from his bank account. Stanley likes to keep things... liquid.

jokes.text: He's more just... Like...
jokes.setup: Julie asking what Michael is usually like
jokes.punchline: Pam's inability to find anything positive to say
jokes.explanation: Pam's struggle to defend Michael reveals there's no better version

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: What is Michael usually like?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Um... he's... he's a great boss. He's really funny. He's very caring. He's... he's a great guy.

jokes.text: Let me tell you something, Oscar, all right? Matt's an okay dude, but he's a dummy. You guys got nothing in common.
jokes.setup: Oscar potentially connecting with Matt
jokes.punchline: Darryl's blunt assessment delivered as friendly advice
jokes.explanation: Darryl's matter-of-fact relationship advice

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Oscar, listen to me. That dude ain't interested in you. And I say that as your friend.

jokes.text: Hi, ladies and gentlemen. My name is Andrew Bernard and I've been on two dates with Erin Hannon and they went well, and there will probably be more. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Andy deciding to reveal their secret relationship
jokes.punchline: Formal announcement like a business presentation
jokes.explanation: Andy's corporate approach to personal announcements is perfectly awkward

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay everyone, if I could have your attention please. I have an announcement to make. This is a formal declaration, so I'm going to need everyone to listen up.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: After careful consideration and extensive deliberation, I am pleased to announce that I am in a relationship. This relationship is effective immediately and will continue indefinitely, pending quarterly reviews.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We have already discussed synergy, aligned on core values, and established a five-year plan. There will be a follow-up meeting next Tuesday to discuss benefits.

jokes.text: I wish he'd come after me. I would be like... / Dwight Schrute! / Dwight Kurt Schrute. Shut up, what?
jokes.setup: Dwight boasting about handling the Scranton Strangler
jokes.punchline: Immediately served with legal papers mid-boast
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - Dwight's tough talk immediately undermined by reality

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I single-handedly captured the Scranton Strangler. The police couldn't do it, but I did. I'm a hero in this town.

dialogue.speaker: Process Server
dialogue.line: Dwight Schrute?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's me.

dialogue.speaker: Process Server
dialogue.line: You've been served.

jokes.text: Dwight recently entered into a contract with me, establishing intent to conceive and raise a child with me. / Did he not tell you that? / What?
jokes.setup: Legal papers being served at the bar
jokes.punchline: Angela revealing the breeding contract to Isabel
jokes.explanation: Angela's public revelation creates maximum awkwardness for everyone

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You've been served.

dialogue.speaker: Isabel
dialogue.line: What? What is this?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: It's a breeding contract. For your cats.

jokes.text: Whack. / You will see me in small claims court!
jokes.setup: Angela demanding an answer about the contract
jokes.punchline: Dwight's single-word response followed by Angela's legal threat
jokes.explanation: Dwight's dismissive 'whack' combined with legal drama escalation

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I need an answer about the contract. Now.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: That's it. I'm calling my lawyer.

jokes.text: Got to hang out with my peeps. Sort of did okay with a new young lady. / Actually you didn't. Not at all.
jokes.setup: Michael's positive assessment of his evening
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate reality check
jokes.explanation: Jim's blunt correction of Michael's self-delusion

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's not what she said.

jokes.text: Some of the credit is due, in fact, to my good friend Date Mike. Nice to meet me.
jokes.setup: Michael still thinking the evening went well
jokes.punchline: Bringing back Date Mike persona as a success
jokes.explanation: Michael's continued delusion about Date Mike's effectiveness

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I think that went really well. I'm bringing back Date Mike. Date Mike is a real person, and people respond to him.

tmdb_overview: Pam and Jim work on their first sales pitch together to Donna, the manager of a local restaurant. But Michael keeps misreading the signals she's putting out. Meanwhile, Dwight encourages Kelly to try out for the minority training program.
insight: Bar setting dilutes The Office's ensemble strength; character comedy carries a softer episode.
